title: 'When Trump declares victory on Iran, the day after will remain an open question'
subhead: The US president will likely quickly proclaim the success of Operation Epic Fury, says Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon, outlining three possible scenarios for the Islamic Republic: Self-dissolution, chaos or the emergence of a so-called 'Venezuelan' model.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

title: 'Municipal elections pose the greatest risks for culture – except in Paris, which enjoys an extraordinary privilege'
subhead: In the French capital, the cultural scene far outpaces that of other major cities, thanks to the state's funding for many museums and opera houses, observes Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin.

title: French cities' steady march toward free public transport
subhead: Ahead of the municipal elections, candidates across the country have promised fare-free transport. Almost 50 cities have already done away with paid tickets, compared to just six before the 2000s.

title: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhead: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
subhead: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
subhead: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.

title: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
subhead: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.
